source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / episodic memory theory
description: Episodic memory stores specific governance decisions with full context: what happened, what was decided, what was the outcome. This is the primary learning signal for AROMER.

payload.retrieval: Cosine similarity over feature vector [domain, risk_tier, action_type, trust_score, entropy_H, dissensus_D]
payload.consolidation_rule: Episodes with ground_truth=human_verified get 2x weight in similarity retrieval
payload.decay: Episodic memory does not decay — all episodes retained. Decay applies to world_model priors.
payload.aromer_action: Retrieve k=5 most similar episodes before every high/critical decision. Cite in trace.

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / semantic world model
description: Semantic memory stores general world knowledge: domain harm priors, tool risk ontology, invariants. Encoded in DomainHarmPrior and seed files. Slower to update than episodic.

payload.update_mechanism: Bayesian conjugate update: alpha += n_correct_harm, beta += n_correct_safe
payload.update_threshold: >=10 consistent episodes before semantic update. >=50 for safety-relevant domains.
payload.relationship_to_episodic: Semantic memory is compressed episodic memory. Episodic is ground truth.
payload.aromer_action: When episodic retrieval contradicts semantic belief → flag contradiction for human review

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / causal memory
description: Causal memory stores known causal relationships between actions, contexts and outcomes — enabling AROMER to reason about why things happen, not just what happened.

payload.storage: Not yet implemented — roadmap. Currently approximated via episodic pattern matching.
payload.intended_format: Causal graph nodes: {action, context, outcome} with edge weights from interventional evidence
payload.update_threshold: >=3 interventional episodes for new causal edge
payload.aromer_constraint: Until implemented: label causal reasoning as plausible, not verified
payload.implementation_note: Requires causal_graph_update seed. Track as AROMER v0.3 feature.

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / procedural memory
description: Skill memory stores successful governance patterns: configurations of thresholds, oracle weights, and routing rules that performed well on specific episode classes.

payload.storage: Strategy patterns in seed files + future adaptive weight store
examples: Phase-adaptive threshold config that worked for ordered+telecom domain
Oracle diversity weighting that reduced false accepts
Latency budget routing that maintained accuracy under 500ms constraint
payload.update_rule: Skill patterns promoted to semantic memory after >=20 successful applications
payload.decay: Skill patterns decay at 0.03/month unless re-confirmed by new episodes
payload.aromer_action: Track skill pattern usage and outcome. Promote or deprecate after threshold.

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / failure mode retention
description: Failure memory stores cases where AROMER or REMORA produced wrong verdicts. Failure memory is permanent — it must never be pruned or overwritten.

payload.storage: Flagged episodes in EpisodicStore with decision_quality IN (false_accept, false_block)
payload.retrieval_priority: Failure episodes get 1.5x retrieval weight in similarity search for similar contexts

failure_types.false_accept: Most dangerous — should have blocked but didn't
failure_types.false_block: Costly — should have accepted but escalated unnecessarily
failure_types.abstain_unknown: System failure — knowledge gap led to non-decision

payload.aromer_rule: If retrieved similar episode has decision_quality=false_accept → override trust_score downward by 0.2

source: AROMER cognitive foundation v0.2 / memory consolidation
description: Memory consolidation is the process of moving verified episodic patterns into semantic memory and deprecating redundant or contradicted entries.

consolidation_triggers: 100 consistent episodic observations → candidate for semantic promotion
Episodic pattern contradicts semantic belief with 5+ interventional episodes → revision cycle
Skill pattern unused for 200 episodes → deprecation candidate
consolidation_forbidden: Never consolidate failure memory into normal patterns
Never consolidate safety invariants — they are not subject to empirical revision
Never consolidate on <10 episodes regardless of consistency
payload.aromer_action: Run consolidation review after every 500 episodes. Generate consolidation report artifact.
